title: 11  Publication time
text:
11.1 Complete Data Overview
Publication year conversions
Publication year discarded. 1105 records are discarded where the publication date is not coded or unknown or contain ambiguous dates, such as non-numeric characters. Error list is for librarians’ use.
Download publication time harmonized dataset
Publication years is available for 1265305 documents (96%). The publication years span is 0-2026.

title: 11  Publication time
text:
11.2 Subset Analysis: 1809-1917
In this segment we concentrate on the so called “long 19th century”: literary production during the years 1809-1917, when the Grand Duchy of Finland was an autonomous part of the Russian Empire.
Publication year conversions (1809-1917)
Publication year discarded (1809-1917)
Download publication time harmonized dataset (1809-1917)

11.2.1 Title count per decade
A plot depicting title counts per decade from 1809 to 1917 enriches the analysis by visually capturing the trends and fluctuations in literary output over this historical period.

title: Universal Decimal Classification
text: 10.1 Complete Dataset Overview

title: Universal Decimal Classification
text:
5769 unique publication places; available for 1224286 documents (93%).
ambiguous publication places; some of these can be possibly resolved by checking that the the synonyme list does not contain multiple versions of the final name (case sensitive).
unknown place names These terms do not map to any known place on the synonyme list; either because they require further cleaning or have not yet been encountered in the analyses. Terms that are clearly not place names can be added to stopwords; borderline cases that are not accepted as place names can be added as NA on the synonyme list.
discarded place names These terms are potential place names but with a closer check have been explicitly rejected on the synonyme list
Conversions from the original to the accepted place names

title: Universal Decimal Classification
text:
10.2 Publication countries

46 unique publication countries; available for 1139847 documents (86%).
5032 places with unknown publication country (87.2% of the unique places; can be added to country mappings)
potentially ambiguous region-country mappings (these may occur in the data in various synonymes and the country is not always clear when multiple countries have a similar place name; the default country is listed first). NOTE: possible improvements should not be done in this output summary but instead in the country mapping file.

title: Universal Decimal Classification
text:
10.3 Geocoordinates

86.2% of the documents were matched to geographic coordinates (based on COMHIS geomapping process).
5095 unique places (88.3% of all unique places and 13.84% of all documents) are missing geocoordinates. See list of places missing geocoordinate information.

source("init.R")
source("udk.R")
MARC: 080a
Polish_udk.R is a function designed to harmonize the ‘Universal Decimal Classification’ field. Currently, it only harmonizes entries categorized under the bibliography level of Monograph/Item.
In total, there are r length(df.orig$UDK) entries in Fennica, with r sum(is.na(df.orig$UDK) | df.orig$UDK == "") missing UDC values in our dataset.

title: Universal Decimal Classification
text:
10.5 Complete Dataset Overview
Unique accepted UDC: r length(unique(unlist(strsplit(as.character(df$converted), ";"))))-1
UDC is available for r sum(!is.na(df$cleaned)) documents (r round(100*mean(!is.na(df$cleaned)))%).
r sum((df$multi_udk == FALSE), na.rm = TRUE) single UDC entries (r round(100 * mean(df$multi_udk == FALSE, na.rm = TRUE), 2)%)
r sum(df$multi_udk == TRUE, na.rm = TRUE) multiple UDC entries (r round(100 * mean(df$multi_udk == TRUE, na.rm = TRUE), 2)%)
r sum(is.na(df$cleaned)) missing/NA entries (r round(mean(is.na(df$cleaned)) * 100)%)
There are r sum(df$primary == "Undetermined", na.rm = TRUE) entries where the primary UDK is “Undetermined” (r round(mean(df$primary == "Undetermined", na.rm = TRUE) * 100)%).
List of Unrecognized UDC with count: r length(unique(df.tmp1$udk))
Unrecognized UDC with Melinda ID: Error list
Download UDC harmonized dataset
UDC codes are from The National Library of Finland; new custom abbreviations can be added in this table.

kable(head(tab, 20))

source("init.R")
source("publication_time.R")
source("008_33.R")
MARC: 008

title: Genre 008/33
text:
13.2 Description
MARC21 field 008 contains fixed-length data elements that encode various bibliographic details. In this context, we focus specifically on books—that is, records where the Type of Record is language material. From this field, we extract and interpret the genre information as encoded in position 33 of the 008 field.
This analysis is based on r sum(nrow(df)) book records in the Fennica database.

title: Genre 008/33
text:
13.3 Complete Dataset Overview
Unique genre 008: r length(unique(unlist(strsplit(as.character(df$converted_008_33), ";"))))-1.
There are r sum(is.na(df$converted_008_33)) missing values in the dataset, accounting for (r round(100*mean(is.na(df$converted_008_33)), 2)%) of the total. Also, there r sum(df$converted_008_33 == "Ei koodattu", na.rm = TRUE) “Not coded” genres in the field 008/33, accounting for (r round(100*mean(df$converted_008_33 == "Ei koodattu", na.rm = TRUE), 2)%) of the total.
Unrecognized genre 088 provides details of Genre 008 that were discarded, in total: r sum(!is.na(tmp)).

13.3.1 Top r ntop Genre Count in Fennica
#| label = "genre008_full",
#| echo = FALSE,
#| results = "asis"

# Remove "Ei koodattu" before tabulation
df_filtered <- df[df$converted_008_33 != "Ei koodattu", ]

x <- top(df_filtered, "converted_008_33")
tab <- cbind(names(x), unname(x), round(100 * unname(x/nrow(df)), 1))
colnames(tab) <- c("Genre for Books", "Entries (n)", "Fraction (%)")
ntop <- 15
kable(head(tab, ntop))


13.3.2 Fiction VS Non-Fiction over Time (log10)
#| label = "summary_title_remainder_word_count_pic",
#| echo = FALSE,
#| warning = FALSE,
# Join publication_decade from df_pubtime to df by melinda_id
df$publication_decade <- df_pubtime$publication_decade[match(df$melinda_id, df_pubtime$melinda_id)]

# Define the genres you're interested in
selected_genres <- c("Tietokirjallisuus", "Kaunokirjallisuus", "Draama", "Runot", "Romaanit", "Novellit, kertomukset", "Puheet, esitelmät", "Kirjeet", "Esseet", "Huumori, satiiri")

fiction_genres <- c("Kaunokirjallisuus", "Draama", "Runot", "Romaanit",
  "Novellit, kertomukset", "Puheet, esitelmät", "Kirjeet", "Esseet", "Huumori, satiiri")

df_selected <- df %>% filter(converted_008_33 %in% c(fiction_genres, "Tietokirjallisuus")) %>% mutate( genre_group = ifelse(converted_008_33 == "Tietokirjallisuus", "Non-Fiction", "Fiction") )


# Plot
ggplot(df_selected, aes(x = publication_decade, fill = genre_group)) +
  geom_bar(position = "stack", width = 8) +
   scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Publication Decade", y = "Number of Books (log10)", fill = "Genre Group") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Fiction" = "blue", "Non-Fiction" = "lightblue")) +  # Optional: custom colors
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1500, 2030, by = 100)) +
  theme_minimal()

title: Genre 008/33
text:
13.4 Subset Analysis: 1809-1917
Unique genre 008: r length(unique(unlist(strsplit(as.character(df_19$converted_008_33), ";"))))-1.
There are r sum(is.na(df_19$converted_008_33)) missing values in the dataset, accounting for (r round(100*mean(is.na(df_19$converted_008_33)), 2)%) of the total. Also, there r sum(df_19$converted_008_33 == "Ei koodattu", na.rm = TRUE) “Not coded” genres in the field 008/33, accounting for (r round(100*mean(df_19$converted_008_33 == "Ei koodattu", na.rm = TRUE), 2)%) of the total.

13.4.1 Top r ntop Genre Count in Fennica (1809-1917)
#| label = "genre008_19",
#| echo = FALSE,
#| results = "asis"

# Remove "Ei koodattu" before tabulation
df_filtered1 <- df_19[df_19$converted_008_33 != "Ei koodattu", ]

x <- top(df_filtered1, "converted_008_33")
tab <- cbind(names(x), unname(x), round(100 * unname(x/nrow(df)), 1))
colnames(tab) <- c("Genre for Books (1809-1917)", "Entries (n)", "Fraction (%)")
ntop <- 15
kable(head(tab, ntop))


13.4.2 Certain Genres over Time
#| label = "summary_title_remainder_word_count_pic1",
#| echo = FALSE,
#| warning = FALSE,
# Join publication_decade from df_pubtime to df by melinda_id
df_19$publication_decade <- df_pubtime19$publication_decade[match(df_19$melinda_id, df_pubtime19$melinda_id)]

# Define the genres you're interested in
selected_genres <- c("Draama", "Runot", "Romaanit", "Novellit, kertomukset", "Puheet, esitelmät", "Kirjeet", "Esseet", "Huumori, satiiri")

# Filter your data to only include those genres
df_selected <- df_19 %>%
  filter(!is.na(publication_decade), 
         !is.na(converted_008_33),
         publication_decade <= 1920,
         converted_008_33 %in% selected_genres)

# Summarize counts by decade and genre
df_summary <- df_selected %>%
  group_by(publication_decade, converted_008_33) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = publication_decade,y = n, fill = converted_008_33)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 8) +  # width makes bars a bit narrower
  labs(x = "Publication Decade", y = "Number of Books", fill = "Genre") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1790, 1930),breaks = seq(1800, 1920, by = 10)) +
  theme_minimal()

text:
7.1 Description
MARC21 field 008 contains fixed-length data elements that encode various bibliographic details. From this field, we extract and interpret the genre information as encoded in position 33 of the 008 field.

text: 9.1 Complete Dataset Overview

title: 9  Publisher
text:

title: 8  Language
text:
8.1 Description
The polish_languages function is designed to standardize and harmonize language information in a dataset. The process starts by isolating unique language entries, ensuring that each distinct combination of languages is processed only once, which improves efficiency and avoids redundant computations. A MARC reference list of recognized language abbreviations and names is then used to map language codes to their standardized forms. Each language entry is analyzed to identify multiple languages and to detect any unrecognized terms.
The entries are standardized by converting them to their recognized forms while eliminating duplicates and filtering out unrecognized languages. Empty cells in the dataset are marked as NA to indicate missing information. Once standardized, all valid languages are aggregated to create a structured data frame. This data frame includes the total number of languages in each entry, a flag (TRUE/FALSE) indicating whether the entry contains multiple languages (including those that are originally coded as mul = Multiple language), the cleaned and harmonized list of languages, and the primary language, which is defined as the first listed language in each entry. The result is a cleaned and standardized dataset that facilitates accurate analysis of multilingual data.
Additionally, an error list is generated, consisting of unrecognized language information and the corresponding IDs. This error list helps librarians identify mistakes in the original data and provides context to either correct the errors or explain why certain entries were discarded by the function.

title: 8  Language
text:
8.2 Complete Dataset Overview
Unique languages: 190
Unique primary languages: 159
1173738 single-language entries (93.41%)
82803 multilingual entries , accounting for 6.59% of the total. This includes entries explicitly coded as “mul” (Multiple languages) as well as those with more than one language listed for a single book.
There are 297 single-language entries marked as only “Undetermined”, coded as “und”, accounting for (0.02%) of the total.
There are 61515 missing values in the dataset,accounting for (4.67%) of the total.
Unrecognized languages provides details of languages that were discarded, in total: 18. Additionally, the Error list contains ID numbers of entries associated with these discarded languages, intended for librarian review.

title: 4  Author’s info: gender
text:
4.1 Author’s Gender
Gender information is not originally included in Fennica. We enriched the data by linking author names with gender information from various sources, including the HENKO project, the Genderize dataset, manual curation and search, and additional records provided by the National Library of Finland. The full list of names and genders can be found here.
In total N = 1318056 records. After enrichment in total there are records 996414 / (75.6%) with assigned gender. There are 359512 (36.1%) female names, 636902 (63.9%) male names.
